cases.esoteric: understood by only a small number of people with special knowledge
cases.demesne: the land attached to a manor, or one's own territory or domain
cases.empyrean: relating to heaven or the sky, celestial
cases.aggrieved: feeling resentment at having been treated unfairly
cases.miasma: an unpleasant or unhealthy vapor, or an oppressive atmosphere
cases.sycophant: a person who acts obsequiously toward someone to gain advantage
cases.fusillade: a series of shots fired or launched at the same time
cases.callous: showing an insensitive disregard for others' feelings
cases.bestial: brutal and savage, like a beast
cases.claustrophobic: having an intense fear of confined spaces
cases.yoke: a burden or restrictive force, or a wooden crosspiece for draft animals
cases.propensity: an inclination or natural tendency to behave in a certain way
cases.pyrrhic victory: a victory won at such great cost it is almost equivalent to defeat
cases.haphazard: lacking any order or organization, random
cases.stave off: to prevent something bad from happening for a period of time
cases.herald: a person or thing that announces or signals the approach of something
cases.awry: away from the expected course, amiss
cases.ineffable: too great or extreme to be expressed in words
cases.tourniquet: a device used to stop blood flow by compressing a limb
cases.jaded: tired, bored, or lacking enthusiasm from overexposure to something
cases.masquerade: to pretend to be someone or something one is not
cases.banality: the quality of being unoriginal or dull, lacking novelty
cases.impunity: freedom from punishment or consequences for one's actions
cases.despondent: in low spirits from loss of hope or courage
cases.reticent: reluctant to speak about one's thoughts or feelings
cases.noxious: harmful or poisonous, especially to health
cases.miscreant: a person who behaves badly or unlawfully
cases.retinue: a group of attendants accompanying an important person
cases.gaunt: thin and haggard, especially from suffering or hunger
cases.alleviate: to make suffering or a problem less severe
cases.virulent: extremely severe or harmful in its effects, or bitterly hostile
cases.cataclysm: a large-scale and violent event, a disaster
cases.exalt: to praise or elevate someone highly
cases.domineering: asserting one's will over others in an arrogant way
cases.pustule: a small blister or pimple containing pus
cases.relegate: to assign someone or something to a lower or less important rank
cases.maelstrom: a powerful, violent whirlpool, or a state of confusion and upheaval
cases.carte blanche: complete freedom to act as one wishes
cases.bedevil: to cause continual trouble for someone
cases.insidious: proceeding in a gradual, subtle way but with harmful effects
cases.malevolence: the desire to cause harm to others, ill will
cases.carrion: the decaying flesh of a dead animal
cases.inoculate: to introduce a substance into the body to protect against disease
cases.unflinching: not showing fear or hesitation, steadfast
cases.wistful: having a feeling of vague longing tinged with melancholy
cases.waif: a homeless or neglected person, especially a child
cases.usurper: a person who takes power or a position illegally or by force
cases.powder keg: a situation that could easily become dangerous or violent
cases.vicissitude: a change of circumstances, especially one that is unpleasant
cases.resigned: having accepted something unpleasant as inevitable
cases.facsimile: an exact copy or reproduction of something
cases.litany: a long, repetitive recital or list of things
cases.firmament: the sky, especially when regarded as a vault or arch
cases.sublime: of such excellence or beauty as to inspire awe
cases.panacea: a solution or remedy for all problems or diseases
cases.unimpeachable: beyond doubt or question, completely trustworthy
cases.sanctity: the state of being holy or sacred
cases.vanquish: to defeat someone thoroughly in battle or contest
cases.fickle: changing frequently, especially in loyalty or affection
cases.tenuous: very weak or slight, barely sufficient
cases.hale: strong and healthy, especially of an older person
cases.decrepit: weakened by age or long use, worn out
cases.decadence: excessive indulgence in pleasure, moral decline
cases.profane: not relating to religion, secular, or showing disrespect for sacred things
cases.iridescent: showing luminous colors that shift when viewed from different angles
cases.suffuse: to gradually spread through or over something
cases.dissonance: a lack of harmony or agreement, especially in sound or ideas
cases.vagary: an unpredictable or erratic change in a situation
cases.stupor: a state of near-unconsciousness or reduced sensibility
cases.rapt: completely absorbed or fascinated by something
